environment.resource_constraint: LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1 is required on this 5.7 GiB host; unconstrained Lake parallelism exhausted memory.

documented_workspace_effect.reason: The env=dev branch adds documentation dependencies in the disposable build workspace. No canonical source or KeyAI-owned external source copy was changed.

build_attempts.command: cd cedar-lean && lake build Cedar SymCC
observations: Concurrent processes emitted secondary failures while reading existing standard-library olean.private files.
The referenced standard-library files were present and readable after the failed attempt.
build_attempts.disposition: Not a project or adapter failure; retry once with the Lean runtime's documented thread limit.

build_attempts.command: cd cedar-lean && LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1 lake build Cedar SymCC

lint_attempts.cause: The upstream lint driver removes exact strings ending in LF. A CRLF checkout therefore reports every present import as missing.

lint_attempts.checkout_policy: core.autocrlf=false, matching committed Git blobs and upstream Linux CI

result.statement: The pinned Cedar and SymCC proof targets build, the upstream import-completeness lint passes under canonical LF checkout semantics, and every source-inventoried public declaration in the compiled closure resolves in Lean with no sorryAx or unexpected custom axiom.
constraints: Use LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1 on this memory-constrained host.
Use canonical LF checkout semantics for the upstream lint driver.
Treat native_decide-generated private axioms as explicit compiler-trust dependencies, not kernel-only evidence.
The documented baseline inherited the host environment and executed reviewed public source; arbitrary untrusted intake remains prohibited until OS-enforced filesystem and network isolation exists.

The compiled probe was launched through the inspected project's lake env, so its output channel is not independently authenticated; a trusted absolute Lean launcher, controlled module search path, loaded-artifact binding, and hostile shadow/output fixture are required before arbitrary untrusted intake.
